dimensions.rationale: Revision 10 is a plan-only write-attribution correction and does not change product requirements.
Controller-owned mutable package manifest handling remains separate from worker-owned implementation paths.

The previously unowned package manifest and Russian README paths now have exact authority attribution; the Russian README moved under the existing release docs/guides scope; the unnecessary root test marker was removed; write overlap remains zero.
The correction does not alter adapter compatibility scope, host protocol behavior, or maturity claims.
No authority, credential, host-operation, neutrality, or release-signing boundary is widened.
Quality metrics remain within the frozen context and validation budgets.
Stdlib discovery remains covered without a root test marker; validation command inventory is unchanged.
Russian README ownership is assigned under the release documentation scope without changing release sequencing.
The controller can now reject unowned writes before task acceptance using the frozen ownership map.
The correction adds one short plan note and no extra required validation invocations.

findings.message: Execution pre-acceptance ownership audit found unowned paths: the mutable package manifest, the root stdlib test-discovery marker, and the Russian README. Revision 10 treats the manifest as plan authority, removes the unnecessary root test marker, and moves the Russian README to docs/guides/README.ru.md under the existing WS-14 release documentation scope.
findings.requiredAction: Preserve exact ownership coverage and fail closed on unowned or forbidden writes before task acceptance.

summary: Revision 10 is READY_TO_FREEZE as a minimal ownership-attribution correction. It changes no requirements, task dependencies, resource budgets, adapter scope, controller gates, or acceptance thresholds.
